reason: Response A has the clipped, observational prose style characteristic of Claude Haiku: short declarative sentences, concrete details without over-explaining, and an understated philosophical turn at the end ("more honest somehow"). Response B reads more like a generic creative writing model imitating "poetic" city description, leaning on flowery metaphors ("dark sentinels," "scattered stars," "breathe with the rhythm") and a more conventional build-up that feels less like Haiku's terse, direct voice.
